"}, {"Note ID": "10207476-RR-72", "Modality": "CT", "Region": "Abdomen", "Exam Name": "CT ABDOMEN W/O CONTRAST", "Report": "TECHNIQUE:

"}, {"Note ID": "10207476-RR-79", "Modality": "CT", "Region": "Abdomen", "Exam Name": "CT ABDOMEN W/O CONTRAST", "Report": "TECHNIQUE:
MDCT-acquired axial images were obtained through the abdomen and

history of contrast allergy.  Coronal and sagittal reformations were
evaluated.

CT OF THE ABDOMEN WITH ORAL CONTRAST:
Included portions of the lung bases
display interval development of a small simple left pleural effusion with
adjacent compressive atelectasis and stable appearance to known coronary
artery calcifications.

Unenhanced images of the liver, spleen, stomach, small bowel, pancreas,
adrenal glands, and kidneys are unremarkable.  There is unchanged appearance
to likely hyperdense biliary sludge and small gallstones noted within the
gallbladder which is not significantly distended.  No intra-abdominal free
fluid, free air, or pathologically enlarged lymph nodes are identified.

CT OF THE PELVIS WITH ORAL CONTRAST ONLY:
In the interval, the patient has
underwent a diverting colostomy which exists in a lower left paramidline
location.  There is extensive colonic diverticulosis again noted, but no
findings to suggest ____.  Contrast has not yet reached the
large bowel and into the colostomy at the time of examination.  Oral contrast

collection is noted to extend from the midline lower pelvis, adjacent to chain

approximately 7 x 8 x 14 cm (300B:56 and 301B:17).  Additional smaller
components of fluid are noted to track into the right anterior pelvic cavity
as well.  Inflammation of the pericolonic fat and omentum is not unexpected
given recent surgery.  Air is noted within the urinary bladder, likely related
to recent instrumentation. No pathologically enlarged inguinal or pelvic
sidewall lymph nodes are identified.  Mild anasarca is noted.

BONE WINDOWS:
No malignant-appearing osseous lesions are identified.
Multilevel degenerative changes within the spine are again noted.

"}, {"Note ID": "10207476-RR-80", "Modality": "Drainage", "Region": "Abdomen", "Exam Name": "CT PERITINEAL DRAIN EXCLUDING APPENDICEAL", "Report": "CT-GUIDED PELVIC ABSCESS DRAINAGE:


PROCEDURE:
Procedure and its complications were explained.  Informed consent
obtained.  Laboratory values checked, INR was initially elevated, fresh frozen
plasma was used to lower this to 1.5.  The patient is also on aspirin and
Plavix, which could not be discontinued because of the cardiac considerations.
It was decided to proceed with the CT-guided abscess drainage.

Timeout was performed.  Area of interest was localized under CT fluoroscopy. 
The skin was prepped and draped in the usual manner.  1% lidocaine used for
local anesthetic.  Conscious sedation was also used.  Under CT fluoroscopic
guidance, a 10 ___ ___ catheter was placed in the left pericolic
abscess.  Approximately 30 cc of thick pus were aspirated.  Specimen has been
sent for microbiology and cell count.  Patient tolerated the procedure well.

small to characterize. No hydronephrosis or concerning renal masses are seen.

bowel wall thickening or obstruction.  Moderate atherosclerotic calcification
is seen in the abdominal aorta, without aneurysmal dilation.

ureters are normal.  There is no pelvic lymphadenopathy. Small amount of air
within the vagina, raises concern for colovaginal fistula.

[{"Note ID": "11662214-RR-10", "Modality": "CT", "Region": "Abdomen", "Exam Name": "CT ABDOMEN W/CONTRAST", "Report": "TECHNIQUE:
Axial images from the lung bases to the symphysis pubis were
obtained after oral and intravenous contrast.

Comparison is made to previous CT dated ___.

FINDINGS:


LUNG BASES:
Small bilateral pleural effusions are seen.  Minimal right lower lobe
atelectasis is present.  No pulmonary nodules or masses are seen.

ABDOMEN:
Within the liver in segment VII, there is a poorly defined low-density lesion,
that measures 10 x 17 mm in diameter.  It is somewhat less well seen on the
previous CT(non-contrast), however, it was present then.  There are no new
liver lesions.  Cholecystectomy clips are seen.  The right kidney is
malrotated, however, no focal abnormality is seen in the right kidney.  The
left kidney is normal.  The spleen, pancreas, and both adrenal glands are
normal with no focal abnormality.

Again free air is seen throughout the peritoneal cavity however there is less
free air today.  No free fluid is seen within the abdomen.  There is
aneurysmal dilatation of the abdominal aorta, which is very focal, with
maximum transverse diameter of 30 x 32 mm in diameter.  There is calcification
of the abdominal aorta.No fat stranding is seen in the region of the duodenum
to suggest a perforated duodenal ulcer.

CT PELVIS:
Again there is significant artifact from the left total hip replacement,
obscuring fine detail.  

An air-filled collection is seen to lie in the left hemipelvis, lateral to the
sigmoid colon.  This measures 25 x 50 mm in diameter, when it previously
measured 16 x 55 mm in diameter.  This is almost completely filled with air
with only a trace of fluid within the dependent portion of this collection. 
The second collection, which lies anterior to the rectum also contains mainly
air with the fluid component also. It previously measured 36 x 56 mm in
diameter, and now measures 55 x 75 mm in diameter.  A third pelvic collecion
lying above the sigmoid colon measures73 x 21 mm in diamter and is nes since
the previous CT.

Three new smaller more anterior fluid collections are seen to lie anterior to
the uterus and above the urinary bladder.  These measure 22 x 23 mm in
diameter, 25 x 72 mm in diameter and 28 x 25 mm in diameter respectively.  The
sigmoid colon is markedly thickened with evidence of diverticular disease.

The dilated loops of small bowel have resolved.  Again, an abdominal wall
hernia seen in the suprapubic region containing small bowel which is not
obstructing.

Urinary bladder contains a urinary catheter within it and is decompressed.  No
large pelvic adenopathy, however, evaluation for adenopathy is limited due to
artifact from the hip prosthesis.

BONES:
There is extensive degenerative disease of the spine.  A wedge
compression fracture of L3 and L1 as well as T12, T10 and T9 are unchanged. 
No new concerning focal bony lesions.

"}, {"Note ID": "11662214-RR-12", "Modality": "CT", "Region": "Abdomen", "Exam Name": "CT ABDOMEN W/O CONTRAST", "Report": "ABDOMEN AND PELVIC CT.:


TECHNIQUE:
MDCT axial images were acquired from the lung bases to the
symphysis pubis, without administration of intravenous contrast.    Long
preparation with oral contrast was performed. Coronal and sagittal
reformations were also performed.

FINDINGS:
There is no evidence of pleural or pericardial effusion.  Coronary
artery calcifications are noted.  Minimal ground-glass opacities are
demonstrated in the lung bases.  Small hiatal hernia is seen.

Non-contrast view of the liver demonstrates a single hypodense lesion in
segment VII (series 2, image 22), which could not be characterized on current
study.  Non-contrast view of the spleen, adrenals and left kidney are
unremarkable.  Right kidney is malrotated without evidence. Visualized bowel
loops are unremarkable.  Pancreas is of normal attenuation and size.  There is
no evidence of retroperitoneal or mesenteric lymphadenopathy.  There is a
stable abdominal aortic aneurysm measuring up to 3 cm.

PELVIC CT:
Urinary bladder is very distended.

Sigmoid colon shows diffuse thickening, correlating to the area of previously
seen perforation.  A drainage catheter is seen in the right lower pelvis with
no residual fluid collection surrounding it.  There is residual fluid
collection in the left lower pelvis, currently measuring approximately 2.3 x
3.7 cm, previously measuring 2.5 x 7.2 cm.

Also seen ventral hernia (series 2, image 66) in the midline pelvis,
containing small bowel loops.  There is no evidence of obstruction or
incarceration.  There is no evidence of free fluid in the pelvis.

Extensive artifacts are seen from the metallic hardware in the hips.

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES:
Diffuse osteopenia is noted with numerous compression
fractures along thoracic and lumbar spine, unchanged from prior studies. Total
hip replacement in the left hip and internal nailing in the right femur.

"}, {"Note ID": "11737161-RR-17", "Modality": "CT", "Region": "Abdomen", "Exam Name": "CT ABD & PELVIS WITH CONTRAST", "Report": "CT OF THE ABDOMEN AND PELVIS WITH IV CONTRAST:


TECHNIQUE:
Multidetector scanning is performed from the diaphragm through the
symphysis during dynamic injection of 130 cc of Optiray.  No prior studies are
available for comparison.

DLP:
903.6 mGy-cm.

CT OF THE ABDOMEN WITH IV CONTRAST:
The lung bases are clear.  In segment VII
of the liver there is a 1.5-cm lesion which is hypodense and measures 58
Hounsfield units.  Emanating from the left portal vein and extending towards
the liver capsule there is a tubular hypodense area which may represent a
thrombosed portal vein branch.  There is some triangularly shaped area of
increased perfusion adjacent to it.  The gallbladder contains a high-density
material possibly vicarious excretion of contrast from prior CT examination. 
The spleen is normal in size.  The pancreas is unremarkable.  The duodenal
diverticulum is noted.  The adrenal glands are normal.  The right kidney is
unremarkable.  In the left kidney there is a mild hydronephrosis and there is
lack of excretion of the left kidney.  Only the cortex is enhancing.  No
contrast is seen in the collecting system as it is seen on the right kidney. 
In the upper pole of the left kidney there is a 0.9 cm hypodense lesion that
is too small to characterize.  The left ureter is mildly distended and can be
followed into the pelvis.  There is some fat stranding surrounding it.

CT OF THE PELVIS WITH IV CONTRAST:
There is massive wall thickening in the
sigmoid colon.  This is relatively hypodense.  In the sigmoid mesentery there
is a small collection that measures 2.5 x 2.3 cm and contains air inclusions. 
It has an enhancing rim and sits superior to the sigmoid colon.  A soft tissue
extending from the sigmoid colon to the bladder with enhancing fistula tract. 
Massive wall thickening of the bladder overlying the fistula tract.  This
measures about 4.0 cm in width and 1.5 cm in depth.  There is air in the
bladder.  The prostate gland is normal in size.  The wall thickening in the
colon is very eccentric and while the normal lumen of the colon is seen
towards the left side of the pelvis the wall thickening extends to the right
side and there is oral contrast seen within this area of the wall consistent
with a sinus tract.

On bone windows, there are no concerning osteolytic or osteosclerotic lesions.

"}, {"Note ID": "11761802-RR-22", "Modality": "MRI", "Region": "Head", "Exam Name": "MR HEAD W & W/O CONTRAST", "Report": "MRI OF THE BRAIN WITHOUT AND WITH CONTRAST, MRA OF THE BRAIN WITHOUT CONTRAST:


AND MRA OF THE NECK WITHOUT AND WITH CONTRAST, ___:


TECHNIQUE:
Routine ___ enhanced MR examination of the brain, comprising
T1-weighted axial SE and sagittal MP-RAGE sequences, post-contrast
administration, the latter with axial and coronal reformations.  Axial 3D- and
2D-time-of-flight MRA of the cervical and cranial vessels, respectively, and
coronal 3D VIBE MRA of the cervical vessels was performed during dynamic
intravenous contrast administration, with review of all axial and coronal
source and subtracted images, respectively, on a separate workstation.  In
addition, axial T1-weighted SE sequence with fat saturation was performed
through the cervical soft tissues, into slab acquisitions, prior to contrast
administration.

FINDINGS:
The study is compared with the cranial NECT and the cervical soft
tissue CECT, performed roughly 17 hours earlier.

Corresponding to the findings on the cranial CT is a somewhat rounded region
of T2- and FLAIR-hyperintensity involving left frontal gray and subjacent
white matter, with mass effect and effacement of the regional gyri.  This
process demonstrates no "blooming" susceptibility artifact to suggest blood
products and an unusual well-defined, ovoid 17 mm (AP), relatively "cystic"
focus, in its peripheral portion.  Allowing for the T2- "shine-through" of
both portions of this process, there is no focus of slow diffusion to suggest
acute ischemia.  The process demonstrates overall intermediate T1 signal,
other than in the more cystic component, above.  Following contrast
administration, there is only at most faint low-level enhancement of the
central portion of this process.  There is no pathologic parenchymal,
leptomeningeal or dural focus of enhancement, elsewhere.

Otherwise, there are only scattered both punctate and larger
T2-/FLAIR-hyperintense foci in bihemispheric, predominantly subcortical white
matter, without diffusion- or enhancing correlate; though nonspecific, these
likely represent sequela of chronic small vessel ischemic disease.  There is
no focus of slow diffusion, elsewhere.

There is normal flow-related enhancement in the included intracranial portions
of both internal carotid and proximal middle and anterior cerebral arteries. 
There is normal, symmetric arborization of MCA branches, with no significant
mural irregularity or flow-limiting stenosis.  There is normal flow-related
enhancement in the distal vertebral arteries, with dominant right and
hypoplastic left vessel, as well as the basilar and bilateral superior
cerebellar and posterior cerebral arteries, without significant mural
irregularity or flow-limiting stenosis.  Anterior and robust bilateral
posterior communicating vessels are demonstrated, with no aneurysm larger than
3 mm.

The axial fat-saturated and T1-weighted sequences demonstrate no abnormal
intramural hyperintensity associated with the cervical, vertebral or carotid
arteries to suggest blood products related to acute dissection.  The included
aortic arch and great vessel origins are unremarkable, without flow-limiting
stenosis.  The common and cervical internal and external carotid arteries are
normal in course, caliber, and contour.  They demonstrate normal flow-related
and contrast enhancement, without significant mural irregularity,
flow-limiting stenosis or evidence of dissection.  Slightly dominant right and
slightly hypoplastic left vertebral arteries demonstrate normal course,
caliber and contour from their subclavian origins through the vertebrobasilar
junction.  There is no significant mural irregularity, flow-limiting stenosis
or evidence of dissection.

"}, {"Note ID": "11761802-RR-29", "Modality": "MRI", "Region": "Head", "Exam Name": "MR FUNCTIONAL BRAIN BY PHYS/PSYCH", "Report": "TECHNIQUE:
Multiplanar T1 and T2 weighted sequences through the brain were
acquired before and after administration of intravenous contrast.  Perfusion
and spectroscopy were performed. Functional MRI was obtained on a 3 Tesla
magnet with Echo Planar/BOLD technique. The functional paradigms include
analysis of the motor areas during the alternating movement of the hands, feet
and tongue.  Language paradigm during the mental process of generating words
with different letters.  Additionally axial MP-RAGE sequence was obtained as a
reference anatomical image, arterial spin label sequence, DTI sequences in
axial projections and tractography with 36 directions was obtained on a
separate workstation.

FINDINGS:
The area of increased FLAIR signal and T2 signal in the left frontal lobe
appears similar compared to prior with an oval cystic component measuring 18
mm in AP dimension and 10 mm in transverse dimension.  Very faint enhancement
just medial to the cystic component is again seen.  Several scattered FLAIR
hyperintense foci in the subcortical white matter suggest sequela of chronic
small vessel ischemic disease, although these are nonspecific.

Major intracranial arteries and venous sinuses appear patent.

Perfusion imaging demonstrates decreased relative cerebral blood volume in the
left frontal abnormality. No significant hyperprfusion is noted on the ASL
sequence.

MR spectroscopy does not include the cystic component of the lesion.  In the
adjacent area of FLAIR signal abnormality, there is uniformly decreased NAA,
which is nonspecific.  There is no definite tumoral spectrum.  Choline and
creatinine peaks are roughly the same in the abnormal and normal areas.

The functional paradigms demonstrate the expected activation areas during the
movement of the hands and feet in the primary motor cortex.  During the
movement of the right hand,  BOLD activation areas are visualized  in close
relation with the left frontal lesion posteriorly (image 5, series 400).  The
language paradigm, demonstrates the majority of the activation on the left
cerebral hemisphere adjacent to the left opercular region, likely related with
dominance,  areas of BOLD signal are visualized posterior to the left frontal 
lesion during the movement of the tongue (image 9, series 600). On the
DTI/Tractography images there is no significant deviation of the association,
commissural and cortico-spinal tracts

"}, {"Note ID": "12145137-RR-65", "Modality": "CT", "Region": "Abdomen", "Exam Name": "CT ABDOMEN W/O CONTRAST", "Report": "TECHNIQUE:
Axial MDCT images were obtained from lung bases to symphysis pubis
with oral contrast administration.  No IV contrast was used.  Sagittal and
coronal reformatted images were then obtained.  The IV contrast was not given
to the patient due to patient's high creatinine.

CT OF THE ABDOMEN WITHOUT IV CONTRAST:
The visualized portion of the lung
bases does not demonstrate any pulmonary nodule, parenchymal opacification or
pleural effusion.  The aortic annulus shows some calcification.  Calcification
of the coronary arteries are also visualized with tiny gas bubbles are
visualized inside the right atrium, most likely related to the patient IV
access..  Large axial hiatal hernia with evidence of reflux is visualized.

The liver is unchanged in appearance.  There is mild intra- and extra-hepatic
biliary ductal dilatation.  The gallbladder is prominent, but there is no
evidence of cholecystitis.

The adrenal glands, spleen, pancreas, stomach, duodenum and loops of small
bowel appear normal. The liver is fatty. Both kidneys are shrunken with no
evidence of hydronephrosis, mass, or stones.  The large bowel loops that are
inside the abdominal cavity also appear normal.  No free fluid is noted within
the abdomen, but there are diffuse locules of air in the intraperitoneal
cavity which also extends along the porta hepatis.  The urinary bladder and
distal ureters appear normal.

CT OF THE PELVIS WITHOUT IV CONTRAST:
The rectum appears normal.  There is
diffuse thickening of the sigmoid colon with ____ which appears to
be perforated with surrounding extraluminal air.  There is also thickening of
the surrounding sigmoid colon mesentery which also contains some gas locules.

The patient is status post thrombectomy of the axillobifemoral and the left
superficial femoral artery bypass.

BONE WINDOWS:
No concerning lytic or sclerotic lesions are visualized.  Mild
ectasia of the suprailiac aorta is noted, measuring up to 21 mm.  The aorta
shows severe calcification.

"}, {"Note ID": "12145137-RR-66", "Modality": "CT", "Region": "Abdomen", "Exam Name": "CT ABDOMEN W/CONTRAST", "Report": "TECHNIQUE:
Axial MDCT images were obtained from the lung bases to the pubic
symphysis with intravenous contrast.  Coronal and sagittal reformatted images
are provided.

CONTRAST:
Oral and intravenous non-ionic contrast were administered.

CT OF THE ABDOMEN WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST:
A 3 mm probably calcified nodule
in the lingula is unchanged.  Note is made of a prominently opacifying pair of
collateral veins traversing the pericardium and coursing to the
retroperitoneum.  The heart and pericardium appear otherwise unremarkable. A
small left pleural effusion is present with adjacent passive atelectasis. The
liver, gallbladder, spleen, pancreas, and adrenal glands appear within normal
limits. There is no hydronephrosis. The kidneys appear somewhat atrophic
bilaterally. The abdominal aorta is normal in caliber proximally, with
prominent mural calcifications consistent with atheromatous disease, and
occludes proximal to the aortic bifurcation. An extra-anatomic bypass graft is
visualized in the subcutaneous tissues of the left flank.

The small bowel is dilated with air fluid levels and a transition point in the
upper pelvis.  Contrast passes to decompressed distal loops, however,
consistent with a partial small-bowel obstruction. Contrast in the colon
probably relates to the previous scan of ___ due to its increased
density.

There is continued colonic mural thickening involving the sigmoid and distal
descending colon, at the site of previously seen ____. A collection
measuring 2.3 x 4.3 cm containing gas and hyperdensity possibly suggestive of
extravasated oral contrast is now present (2:54, 57) just adjacent to the site
where extraluminal gas indicated perforation on the prior study.

CT OF THE PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST:
The bladder contains air,
suggestive of possible Foley catheter insertion although clinical correlation
is necessary.  There is increased edema of the sigmoid colon in comparison
with ___.  A small fluid collection measuring 2.4 x 4.1 cm with
peripheral enhancement is seen just adjacent to the rectum (2:70), new since
the previous examination.  Extra-anatomic bypass grafts (axillary- femoral and
cross-femoral), opacify symmetrically.  There are no pathologically enlarged
pelvic or inguinal lymph nodes.

BONE WINDOWS:
Bone windows show old healed fractures of left-sided ribs and
degenerative change of the thoracic and lumbar spine with no lesions worrisome
for osseous metastatic disease.

"}, {"Note ID": "12145137-RR-69", "Modality": "Drainage", "Region": "Abdomen", "Exam Name": "CT PERITINEAL DRAIN EXCLUDING APPENDICEAL", "Report": "PROCEDURE:
The risks and benefits of the procedure were explained to the
patient, and written informed consent was obtained.  A preprocedure timeout
was performed using two patient identifiers.  The patient was placed supine on
the CT table.  Initial scanning again showed a collection in the left lower
quadrant, adjacent to the colon, containing air and oral contrast and
measuring 5.7 x 4.8 cm.  The skin of the left lower quadrant was prepped and
draped in standard sterile fashion.  After local anesthesia with 6 cc of 1%
lidocaine, and direct CT guidance, a 19-gauge ___ needle was advanced into
the collection in the left lower quadrant and a 0.038 ___ wire advanced
into the cavity after visualization of a small amount of pus draining from the
needle.  An 8 ___ dilator was then passed over the wire and an 8 ___
pigtail catheter advanced into the collection.  The pigtail was formed and
locked and 20 cc of purulent sanguinous material aspirated and sent to
microbiology.  The catheter was placed to external bag for continued
draininage and was secured to the skin with a StatLock device. The patient
tolerated the procedure well, with no complications evident at the time of the
procedure. The attending radiologist, Dr. ___, was present and supervising
throughout the procedure.

MODERATE SEDATION:
The patient received 100 mcg of fentanyl and 1 mg of
Versed in divided doses for moderate sedation during a total intraservice time
of 25 minutes, during which time, the patient's hemodynamic parameters were
continuously monitored.

"}, {"Note ID": "13150383-RR-17", "Modality": "Drainage", "Region": "Abdomen", "Exam Name": "CT PERITINEAL DRAIN EXCLUDING APPENDICEAL", "Report": "PROCEDURE:
CT-guided percutaneous left lower quadrant abscess drainage.

ANESTHESIA:
50 mcg fentanyl IV and 1 mg Versed IV.

TECHNIQUE:
The benefits, risks, and alternatives of the procedure were
discussed with the patient.  The patient's questions were answered and
written, informed consent was obtained.  The patient was brought into the CT
procedure suite and placed in a right lateral semi-decubitus position. 
Initial CT images were obtained to determine a safe access site.  The access
site was marked on the skin.  The skin was prepped and draped in usual sterile
fashion.  Local lidocaine anesthesia was administered.  Using CT fluoroscopic
guidance, an 18-gauge ___ needle was advanced into the left lower quadrant
abscess.  The inner stylet was removed.  A 0.035 ___ guidewire was
inserted through the needle into the abscess.  The needle was removed.  An 8
___ Bard drainage catheter was advanced into the collection over the
guidewire.  The guidewire was removed.  The pigtail loop of the catheter was
locked.  The catheter was affixed to the skin with StatLock adhesive device. 
Approximately 40 mL of purulent material was aspirated from the collection. 
The catheter was left to gravity drainage.  Fluid was sent for Gram stain and
culture.  The patient tolerated the procedure well.  No immediate
complications occurred.

FINDINGS:
Initial CT images demonstrate a 5.2 x 4.5 cm left lower quadrant
abscess containing gas and fluid adjacent to the sigmoid colon.  The sigmoid
colon wall is thickened.  Inflammatory changes involve the surrounding fat. 
Subsequent CT fluoroscopic images demonstrate the needle and the catheter tip
within the collection.  Retained oral contrast is present within the large
bowel.

"}]

"}, {"Note ID": "14258834-RR-24", "Modality": "CT", "Region": "Abdomen", "Exam Name": "CTA ABD & PELVIS", "Report": "TECHNIQUE:
Multidetector CT axial images were acquired through the abdomen
and pelvis prior to and after the administration of IV contrast. Postcontrast
imaging was performed in the arterial and portal venous phase.
Coronal and sagittal reformations were performed and submitted to PACS for
review.
No oral contrast was administered.

DOSE:
DLP: 3595.37 mGy-cm (abdomen and pelvis.
IV Contrast:  150 mL Omnipaque

FINDINGS:


LOWER CHEST:
Limited evaluation of the lung bases demonstrates minimal basilar atelectasis.

ABDOMEN:


HEPATOBILIARY:
The liver demonstrates homogenous attenuation throughout. 
There is no evidence of focal lesions.  There is no evidence of intrahepatic
or extrahepatic biliary dilatation.  The gallbladder is within normal limits,
without stones or gallbladder wall thickening.

PANCREAS:
The native pancreas is atrophic in appearance.  There is no evidence
of pancreatic ductal dilatation or mass.  There is a transplant pancreas in
the left pelvis. There is fatty stranding around the transplant pancreas
compatible with pancreatitis, as seen on the prior exam. The transplant
pancreas parenchyma enhances homogeneously without evidence of necrosis. 
There are reactive peripancreatic lymph nodes.

SPLEEN:
The spleen shows normal size and attenuation throughout, without
evidence of focal lesions.

ADRENALS:
The right and left adrenal glands are normal in size and shape.

URINARY:
The native kidneys are atrophic in appearance. There is a right
pelvic transplant kidney which demonstrates normal enhancement. There is no
hydronephrosis, solid renal mass, or renal calculus. There is a 5 mm
hypodensity in the superior pole of the transplant kidney which is too small
to characterize but likely represents a small cyst. The bladder is distended
but normal.

GASTROINTESTINAL:
In the left pelvis at the site of transplant pancreatitis,
there is a loop of small bowel which is mildly dilated and thick-walled,
likely representing local ileus from adjacent inflammation. The remainder of
the small bowel has a normal appearance. Colon and rectum are within normal
limits. Appendix contains air, has normal caliber without evidence of fat
stranding.

RETROPERITONEUM:
There is no evidence of retroperitoneal and mesenteric
lymphadenopathy.

VASCULAR:
There is no abdominal aortic aneurysm.  There is minimal calcium
burden in the abdominal aorta and great abdominal arteries.  The left gastric
artery and celiac artery share a common origin off the aorta. There is a
replaced left hepatic artery arising from the left gastric artery.

PELVIS:
The urinary bladder and distal ureters are unremarkable.  There is no evidence
of pelvic or inguinal lymphadenopathy.  There is no free fluid in the pelvis. 
There are implants in the bilateral corpus cavernosum.

BONES AND SOFT TISSUES:
There is no evidence of worrisome lesions. Abdominal and pelvic wall is within
normal limits.

[{"Note ID": "14905634-RR-84", "Modality": "CT", "Region": "Abdomen", "Exam Name": "CTA ABD W&W/O C & RECONS", "Report": "TECHNIQUE:

A collection of free air is noted anterior to the liver, and
outlining bowel loops in the right upper quadrant.  At the root of the
mesentery, a few locules of gas are also present, along with a small amount of

gutters, and pelvis.

The stomach and small bowel are unremarkable.  Colonic diverticulosis is noted
without definite evidence of diverticilis. There is no bowel dilation to
suggest obstruction.  No abnormal wall thickening, enhancement, pneumatosis,

The liver enhances homogeneously, without focal masses.  Stable intrahepatic
biliary dilatation is noted, secondary to prior cholecystectomy.  There is

[{"Note ID": "15136747-RR-26", "Modality": "CT", "Region": "Abdomen", "Exam Name": "CT ABD & PELVIS WITH CONTRAST", "Report": "TECHNIQUE:

obtained after the administration of intravenous contrast. No oral contrast

842 mGy-cm.

identified. There is no intrahepatic biliary dilatation. The portal vein is
patent. The gallbladder is without radiopaque cholelithiasis. The common bile
duct is prominent measuring 9 mm (2:29 and 601b:35).  The pancreas is without
pancreatic duct dilatation. No focal lesion is identified. The spleen is
normal in size. An accessory spleen is incidentally noted. Adrenal glands
bilaterally are within normal limits and without nodularity. The kidneys
present symmetric nephrograms and excretion of contrast. Extending from the
posterior interpolar region of the left renal cortex, there is 8.6 x 6.6 cm

the left distal ureter becomes dilated with surrounding inflammatory changes
(2:71).  There is a 7.6 x 8.3 mm high density focus at the left ureteropelvic

approximately 3.0 x 3.3 cm extending from the second portion (2:33). Loops of
small bowel are unremarkable. The appendix is air-filled and unremarkable.

there is a approximate 15 cm loop of colon with bowel wall thickening and

"}, {"Note ID": "15244957-RR-58", "Modality": "Drainage", "Region": "Abdomen", "Exam Name": "CT PERITINEAL DRAIN EXCLUDING APPENDICEAL", "Report": "FINDINGS:
A limited low-dose CT scan performed through the pelvis to plan the procedure.
Again identified is a 8.4 cm fluid collection with air-fluid level adjacent to
the sigmoid colon in the midline pelvis.  The bladder was decompressed from
the Foley catheter.

PROCEDURE:
Prior imaging was reviewed.  A focused physical exam was performed on the
patient.  Patient's coagulation parameters were checked, and deemed to be
appropriate.  Written informed consent from the patient was obtained,
detailing the benefits and risks of the procedure.  Pre-procedure time-out was
taken confirming the patient details and proposed procedure.  

The patient was placed in supine position and prepped and draped in the usual
fashion.  1% lidocaine was used for local anesthetic.  Under CT guidance an 18
gauge ___ needle was advanced into the abscess cavity using an anterior
abdominal wall midline approach.  Purulent material was aspirated.  A 10 gauge
___ catheter was then advanced into the abscess cavity using trocar
technique.  Approximately 120 cc of purulent material was drained, and sent to
microbiology for evaluation.  The catheter was attached to a drainage bag.  A
statlock and adhesive gauze was used to secure the catheter. 

Moderate sedation was provided by administering divided doses of Versed (total
dosage 1 mg) for the procedure during which the patient hemodynamic parameters
were continuously monitored.  

No complications were encountered.

"}, {"Note ID": "15353817-RR-61", "Modality": "CT", "Region": "Abdomen", "Exam Name": "CT ABD & PELVIS WITH CONTRAST", "Report": "EXAMINATION:
CT abdomen and pelvis with and without contrast

TECHNIQUE:
Single phase split bolus contrast: MDCT axial images were acquired
through the abdomen and pelvis following intravenous contrast administration
with split bolus technique. IV Contrast:  130 mL Omnipaque.
Coronal and sagittal reformations were performed and reviewed on PACS.
Oral and Rectal contrast were administered.

DOSE:
This study involved 4 CT acquisition phases with dose indices as
follows:
   1) CT Localizer Radiograph
   2) CT Localizer Radiograph
   3) Stationary Acquisition 4.0 s, 0.5 cm; CTDIvol = 19.3 mGy (Body) DLP =
9.6 mGy-cm.
   4) Spiral Acquisition 6.2 s, 67.9 cm; CTDIvol = 16.2 mGy (Body) DLP =
1,102.6 mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 1,112 mGy-cm.

FINDINGS:


LOWER CHEST:
Bilateral pleural effusions greater on the right versus left with
compressive atelectasis.  No evidence of loculation.

ABDOMEN:


HEPATOBILIARY:
Morphological changes of cirrhosis are present with a nodular
hepatic contour and enlarged preportal space.  There is no evidence of focal
lesions.  There is no evidence of intrahepatic or extrahepatic biliary
dilatation.  The gallbladder is distended with inspissated bile.  The
gallbladder wall shows normal attenuation.  There is no pericholecystic fluid.

PANCREAS:
The pancreas has normal attenuation throughout, without evidence of
focal lesions or pancreatic ductal dilatation.  There is no peripancreatic
stranding.

SPLEEN:
There is splenomegaly, likely secondary to portal hypertension from
cirrhosis.

ADRENALS:
The right and left adrenal glands are normal in size and shape.

URINARY:
The kidneys are of normal and symmetric size with normal nephrogram. 
There is no evidence of focal renal lesions or hydronephrosis.  There is no
perinephric abnormality.

VASCULAR:
There is no abdominal aortic aneurysm.  Mild atherosclerotic disease
is noted.

GASTROINTESTINAL:
Patient is status post sigmoid colectomy with diverting
loop ileostomy.  A surgical drain below diaphragm is noted.  Small volume
ascites is present.  There is a small fluid collection abutting the posterior
abdominal wall, measuring approximately 3.9 x 2.4 cm, with homogeneous
attenuation and a thin well-defined border, likely consistent with
postoperative seroma.  There is no evidence of an anastomotic leak or
obstruction.  Small bowel loops demonstrate normal caliber, wall thickness,
and enhancement throughout.  The remaining colon and rectum are within normal
limits.  A rectal tube is present.  The appendix is normal.

PELVIS:
There is fluid in the right inguinal canal along the spermatic cord. 
The urinary bladder and distal ureters are unremarkable.  There is no free
fluid in the pelvis.

REPRODUCTIVE ORGANS:
The reproductive organs are unremarkable.

LYMPH NODES:
At the aortic bifurcation on the left, there is a nonspecific
density, likely lymph node (series 2, image 81).  Scattered mesenteric
lymphadenopathy, none pathologically enlarged by CT size criteria.  There is
no retroperitoneal, pelvic or inguinal lymphadenopathy.

BONES AND SOFT TISSUES:
There is no evidence of worrisome osseous lesions or
acute fracture. The abdominal and pelvic wall is within normal limits.

"}, {"Note ID": "15610631-RR-41", "Modality": "Ultrasound", "Region": "Abdomen", "Exam Name": "LIVER OR GALLBLADDER US (SINGLE ORGAN)", "Report": "ABDOMINAL ULTRASOUND:

complex cystic lesion with multiple internal septations is noted in the liver

demonstrate arterial flow as demonstrated by spectral analysis.

Otherwise, the remaining of the liver is unremarkable.  There is no
intrahepatic biliary duct dilatation.  The gallbladder demonstrates a 0.4 xm
polyp in its neck but no wall thickening or cholelithiasis. The common bile
duct is not dilated, measuring 6 mm. The portal vein is patent with
hepatopetal flow. The head and body of the pancreas are unremarkable. The
pancreatic tail is not visualized due to bowel gas artifact. The spleen is
normal in size, 7.6 cm.  Limited views of the aorta and inferior vena cava are
unremarkable.  There is no ascites.  

The right kidney measures 10.8 cm and the left kidney measures 10.9 cm.  The
right kidney is within normal limits, without evidence of hydronephrosis,
nephrolithiasis, or focal lesion.  There is normal corticomedullary
differentiation.  In the upper pole of the left kidney, there is 3.8 x 3.3 x
3.2 cm cystic lesion with one thin internal septation slightly  increased in

"}, {"Note ID": "15610631-RR-42", "Modality": "MRI", "Region": "Spine", "Exam Name": "MR CERVICAL SPINE W/O CONTRAST", "Report": "TECHNIQUE:
Sagittal T1, T2, STIR and axial T2 weighted images were acquired
through the  cervical, thoracic, and lumbar spine without intravenous contrast
due to acute renal failure.

FINDINGS:
Cervical spine:  Vertebral bodies are normal in signal intensity and
alignment.  There is mild height loss of the C5 and C6 vertebral bodies, which
is likely chronic given the absence of stir hyperintensity.  The spinal cord
is normal in signal characteristics.  Visualized posterior fossa structures
are normal.  There is disc desiccation and loss of intervertebral disc height
throughout the cervical spine.  No fluid collection is identified.  

At C2-C3, there is a broad-based disc protrusion without significant cananl or
foraminal narrowing.

At C3-C4, there is a central disc protrusion all which mildly narrows the
spinal canal.  Facet and uncovertebral joint degenerative changes results in
bilateral moderate neural foraminal narrowing.  

At C4-C5, there is a left paracentral disc protrusion which contacts and
indents the spinal cord without is cord signal abnormality.  There is mild to
moderate right neural foraminal narrowing and mild left neural foraminal
narrowing. 

At C5-C6, there is a large broad-based disc protrusion with a superimposed
left paracentral protrusion with an annular tear which results in deformity of
the spinal cord with effacement of the CSF spaces.  There is severe spinal
canal narrowing.  Facet and uncovertebral joint osteophytes result in
bilateral severe neural foraminal narrowing. 

At C6-C7, there is a broad-based disk protrusion eccentric to the left which
results in spinal cord flattening and remodeling without signal abnormality. 
There is moderate spinal canal narrowing and no significant neural foraminal
narrowing.  

The C7-T1 level is unremarkable.  

The paravertebral soft tissues are normal.  

Thoracic spine:  There is convex left scoliotic curvature.  The vertebral
bodies are normal in height, signal intensity, and alignment.  There is disc
desiccation and loss of disc height, with paracentral disc protrusions at
T2-T3, T3-T4, and T5-T6 largest at T3-T4.  There is no fluid collection.  The
spinal cord is normal in signal intensity and caliber.  There is no
significant spinal canal or neural foraminal stenosis.  

Lumbar spine: the vertebral bodies are normal in height and alignment. There
are degenerative endplate marrow signal changes at L5-S1, ___ type 2.  There
is disc desiccation and loss of intervertebral disc height. The distal spinal
cord and conus medullaris is normal in appearance, with the conus terminating
at approximately the L2 level. 

There is a partially evaluated disc bulge at L2-L3 which results in mild
spinal canal narrowing and mild right neural foraminal narrowing. 

At L3-L4, there is a diffuse disc bulge, ligamentum flavum thickening, facet
degenerative changes resulting in mild spinal canal narrowing, mild to
moderate right neural foraminal narrowing, and mild left neural foraminal
narrowing. 

At L4-L5, there is a diffuse disc bulge as well as a 2.2 x 1.1 cm superiorly
migrated structure which appears contiguous with the adjacent disc and is
similar in signal characteristics to the disc. This therefore likely
represents an extruded disc fragment. It is more prominent than on prior. In
combination with ligamentum flavum thickening and facet degenerative changes,
these findings result in severe spinal canal narrowing with near complete
obliteration of the thecal sac, moderate right neural foraminal narrowing, and
moderate left neural foraminal narrowing.  

At L5-S1, there is a diffuse disc bulge with central protrusion, ligamentum
flavum thickening, and facet degenerative change resulting in mild spinal
canal narrowing, moderate right neural foraminal narrowing, and severe left
neural foraminal narrowing.  This is similar to prior exam. 

The paravertebral soft tissues are unremarkable. Multiple T2 hyperintense
structures are noted in the left kidney most likely representing cysts.

"}, {"Note ID": "15610631-RR-50", "Modality": "CT", "Region": "Abdomen", "Exam Name": "CT ABD & PELVIS WITH CONTRAST", "Report": "TECHNIQUE:
MDCT acquired 5 mm axial images of the abdomen and pelvis were
obtained following the uneventful administration of oral contrast and 130 cc
of Omnipaque intravenous contrast.  Coronal and sagittal reformats were
generated.

Total exam DLP:  833.78 mGy-cm.

FINDINGS:
There are small nonhemorrhagic pleural effusions in the bases bilaterally.
There is associated atelectasis in the right lung base.  There are
calcifications in the coronary arteries.

CT OF THE ABDOMEN:
There is a pigtail drainange catheter terminating within a
7.0 x 6.1 x 3.5 cm heterogeneous collection in the right hepatic lobe (2:19
and 300b:28).  Otherwise, the liver enhances homogeneously.  There is no
intrahepatic biliary duct dilatation.  The gallbladder is unremarkable and the
portal vein is patent.  There is free intraabdominal air and pockets of air
are seen along the ligamentum venosum. The spleen is normal in size. There is
a low attenuation lesion in the anterior aspect of the spleen, too small to
characterize.  The pancreas does not demonstrate focal lesions, peripancreatic
stranding or fluid collection.  The adrenal glands are unremarkable.  There
are two hypodense lesions in the left kidney, the largest of which measures
3.1 x 3.3 cm, likely represents a cyst (2:31).  Otherwise, the kidneys enhance
symmetrically and excrete contrast without evidence of hydronephrosis or mass.


The stomach, duodenum and small bowel are grossly unremarkable.  The intra
abdominal aorta and its branches demonstrate atherosclerotic calcifications. 
There is no mesenteric or retroperitoneal lymph node enlargement by CT size
criteria.

CT OF THE PELVIS:
There are extensive diverticula throughout the sigmoid
colon. A 3.0 cm collection of air and fluid is identified in the pericolic
region of the sigmoid colon (2:73). There is associated bowel wall thickening.
The urinary bladder and terminal ureters are normal.  No pelvic or inguinal
lymph node enlargement is seen.  There is no pelvic free fluid.

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES:
There are degenerative changes along the thoracic and
lumbar spine most prominent at the T12-L1 and L5-S1 with anterior osteophyte
formation.  No blastic or lytic lesions suspicious for malignancy is present

"}, {"Note ID": "15653234-RR-24", "Modality": "CT", "Region": "Abdomen", "Exam Name": "CT ABD & PELVIS WITH CONTRAST", "Report": "EXAMINATION:
CT abdomen and pelvis.

TECHNIQUE:
MDCT axial images were acquired through the abdomen and pelvis
following intravenous contrast administration with split bolus technique.
Coronal and sagittal reformations were performed and reviewed on PACS.
Oral contrast was administered.

DOSE:
This study involved 5 CT acquisition phases with dose indices as
follows:
   1) CT Localizer Radiograph
   2) CT Localizer Radiograph
   3) Stationary Acquisition 0.5 s, 0.5 cm; CTDIvol = 4.8 mGy (Body) DLP = 2.4
mGy-cm.
   4) Stationary Acquisition 6.5 s, 0.5 cm; CTDIvol = 62.6 mGy (Body) DLP =
31.3 mGy-cm.
   5) Spiral Acquisition 6.4 s, 69.5 cm; CTDIvol = 16.3 mGy (Body) DLP =
1,132.3 mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 1,166 mGy-cm.

FINDINGS:


LOWER CHEST:
Please refer to separate report of CT chest performed on the same
day for description of the thoracic findings.

ABDOMEN:


HEPATOBILIARY:
The liver demonstrates homogenous attenuation throughout. 
There is no evidence of focal lesions.  Punctate right hepatic hypodensities
are too small to characterize but likely cysts or biliary hamartomas.  There
is no evidence of intrahepatic or extrahepatic biliary dilatation. The
gallbladder is within normal limits, without stones or gallbladder wall
thickening.

PANCREAS:
The pancreas has normal attenuation throughout, without evidence of
focal lesions or pancreatic ductal dilatation.  Punctate calcifications within
the pancreatic tail are noted, and suggest prior chronic pancreatitis.  There
is no peripancreatic stranding.

SPLEEN:
The spleen shows normal size and attenuation throughout, without
evidence of focal lesions.

ADRENALS:
Parent bulk fat containing lesion at the superior aspect of the
right adrenal gland likely right-sided myelolipoma measures 1.8 x 1.2 cm.  The
bilateral adrenal glands are otherwise unremarkable appearance.

URINARY:
The kidneys are of normal and symmetric size with normal nephrogram. 
There is no evidence of hydronephrosis. Multiple bilateral renal cysts measure
up to 3.8 cm in the midportion of the left kidney.  An intermediate density
lesion in the medial left upper pole measuring 1.4 cm is present.  This was
also partly visualized on previous lumbar spine MRI and a low signal on T2
lesion.  This may represent a chronic hemorrhagic or proteinaceous cyst.

GASTROINTESTINAL:
A small hiatal hernia is noted.  Small bowel loops
demonstrate normal caliber, wall thickness and enhancement throughout. 
Colonic diverticulosis is noted, with focal fat stranding and wall thickening
involving a segment of sigmoid colon compatible with ____. 
Immediately adjacent to this segment of colon, there is a 5.1 x 4.9 cm
phlegmonous collection (series 2, image 101, extending superiorly to the left
flank (series 2, image 92).

RETROPERITONEUM:
There is no evidence of pathologic retroperitoneal
lymphadenopathy.

VASCULAR:
There is no abdominal aortic aneurysm. There is moderate calcium
burden in the abdominal aorta and great abdominal arteries.  Focal mural
plaque on the right lateral wall of the abdominal aorta noted on series 2,
image 70).  1.7 cm left, 1.5 cm right common iliac artery aneurysms are
present.

PELVIS:
The urinary bladder is collapsed with mild wall thickening and
adjacent reactive fat stranding from the inflamed sigmoid colon. There is no
evidence of pelvic or inguinal lymphadenopathy. There is no free fluid in the
pelvis.

BONES AND SOFT TISSUES:
There is no evidence of worrisome osseous lesions.  A
small ventral hernia contains a loop of large bowel, without evidence of
strangulation or obstruction.  Multiple additional fat containing ventral
hernias are noted.

NOTIFICATION:
Acute findings were paged by Dr. ___ to Dr. ___, at
23:21 on ___.

"}, {"Note ID": "15653234-RR-29", "Modality": "CT", "Region": "Abdomen", "Exam Name": "CT ABD & PELVIS WITH CONTRAST", "Report": "EXAMINATION:
CT abdomen and pelvis with IV contrast.

TECHNIQUE:
MDCT axial images were acquired through the abdomen and pelvis
following intravenous contrast administration with split bolus technique.
Coronal and sagittal reformations were performed and reviewed on PACS.
Oral contrast was administered.

DOSE:
Total DLP (Body) = 896 mGy-cm.
IV Contrast:  150 mL Omnipaque.

FINDINGS:
There is minimal dependent atelectasis.  Tiny bibasilar consolidations are
minimally changed since the ___ examination, possibly
aspiration-related.  The heart size is normal.  There is no pericardial or
pleural effusion.

The liver density is within normal limits.  No discrete hepatic mass is
detected.  There is no intra or extrahepatic bile duct dilation.  The
gallbladder is normal.

The spleen size is normal.

The pancreas demonstrates normal density and bulk.  There is no pancreatic
duct dilation.

The left adrenal gland is normal.  Arising from the superior aspect of the
right adrenal gland is a well-circumscribed 17 x 14 mm lesion demonstrating
bulk fat, compatible with a myelolipoma (series 2, image 20).  Multiple
hypodense well-circumscribed benign-appearing cysts are seen throughout both
kidneys, the largest arising from the upper pole of the right kidney measuring
4.6 cm (series 2, image 25, 30, 35).  Other subcentimeter hypodense lesions
are also likely cysts, but remain too small for complete characterization. 
There is no collecting system obstruction.

A small hiatal hernia is present (series 2, image 15).  The stomach and
intra-abdominal loops of small and large bowel are normal in caliber.

Mild soft and calcific atherosclerotic plaques are demonstrated throughout the
abdominal aorta and iliac branches, without dissection, or flow limiting
stenosis.  The celiac trunk, SMA, renal arteries, and ___ are patent and
normal in caliber.  An accessory right renal artery is incidentally noted
(series 2, image 37, 34).

There is no mesenteric or retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy.

A percutaneous catheter previously terminating within a pelvic collection has
been retracted, now terminating near the anterior abdominal wall, and no
longer communicating with the collection (series 2, image 68).  The collection
itself is markedly smaller in size since the pre drainage CT examination from
___, now appearing as a curvilinear pocket of gas spanning up to 5.3
cm, measuring up to 1 cm in thickness (series 601b, image 32, series 2, image

"}, {"Note ID": "16117248-RR-71", "Modality": "CT", "Region": "Abdomen", "Exam Name": "CT ABD & PELVIS WITH CONTRAST", "Report": "TECHNIQUE:

evaluation of supradiaphragmatic contents, the visualized lung bases show
posterior dependent positional changes but no pleural effusion, focal
consolidation, or pulmonary nodules.  Limited evaluation of the heart shows no
pericardial effusion.  The descending thoracic aorta shows extensive atheroma
and calcified plaque narrowing the vessel lumen (2:5).

The liver enhances homogeneously without perfusion defects or focal liver
lesions.  The portal venous system opacifies satisfactorily with contrast.  No
intra- or extra-hepatic biliary dilation is seen.  The patient is status post
cholecystectomy.  A right-sided ventricular shunt catheter is unchanged in
position with the tip terminating anterior to the liver.  The pancreas is
fatty infiltrated but otherwise unremarkable.  The spleen and bilateral
adrenal glands are unremarkable.  Both kidneys enhance symmetrically and
excrete contrast normally without evidence of hydronephrosis or solid renal
mass.  The left kidney contains numerous subcentimeter hypodensities in the
cortex which are too small to fully characterize by CT.

The stomach and intra-abdominal loops of small and large bowel are
unremarkable without evidence of wall thickening or obstruction.  No free air
or ascites is present.  There is extensive atherosclerotic disease of the
descending aorta with ectasia of the distal infrarenal portion measuring 2.5 x
2.3 cm (2:41), which is not significantly changed from ___.  An
uncomplicated 6.5 cm fat-containing ventral hernia is noted through which the
VP shunt courses.

CT PELVIS WITH CONTRAST:
The patient is status post hysterectomy.  The
urinary bladder and rectum are unremarkable.  A left likely ovarian cyst is
increased in size from both prior studies, now measuring 6.2 x 3.6 cm (2:63),
which previously measured 3.0 x 2.5 cm in ___ and 1.5 x 1.1 cm in ___.  The
sigmoid colon contains multiple diverticula and a 5.5 x 4.2 x 3.1 cm rim
enhancing thick-walled collection of air and fecalized material with no

is closely approximated to the left posterior dome of the urinary bladder and

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES:

multilevel severe degenerative changes in the lumbar spine with loss of

suspicious lytic or sclerotic lesions are identified in the bone.

complications.  The patient was returned to her inpatient bed in good
condition.

"}, {"Note ID": "19689280-RR-14", "Modality": "CT", "Region": "Abdomen", "Exam Name": "CT ABDOMEN W/O CONTRAST", "Report": "TECHNIQUE:

after only administration of water-soluble oral contrast (Gastrografin).  No

pleural effusion compared to ___, but moderate decrease from the
original study in ___.  Trace right pleural effusion is noted.

The absence of IV contrast significantly limits evaluation of intra-abdominal
organs.  Allowing for the limitations, the liver is grossly normal.  There are
at least two small, partially calcified gallstones in a non-distended
gallbladder, without secondary signs for acute cholecystitis.  The stomach,
duodenum, and visualized loops of small bowel are normal and patent with oral
contrast.  The adrenal glands and kidneys are grossly normal.  There is no
evidence of hydronephrosis, hydroureter or renal stone.  The patient is status

There is no free intra-abdominal air beyond the collection.  No free fluid is

BONE WINDOW:

unchanged grade 2 anterolisthesis of L4 on L5.  Metallic density posterior to

"}, {"Note ID": "19689280-RR-19", "Modality": "CT", "Region": "Head", "Exam Name": "CT HEAD W/O CONTRAST", "Report": "TECHNIQUE:

"}, {"Note ID": "19689280-RR-20", "Modality": "CT", "Region": "Chest", "Exam Name": "CT CHEST W/CONTRAST", "Report": "TECHNIQUE:
MDCT images were acquired through the chest, abdomen, and pelvis
after administration of oral and 130 cc of Optiray intravenous contrast. 
Sagittal and coronal reformats were generated and reviewed.

CT OF THE CHEST WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST:
The major airways are patent up to
subsegmental levels bilaterally.  The endotracheal tube ends 3.7 cm above the
carina.  Right IJ line and right PICC end in the mid SVC. Bilateral pleural
effusions are present, moderate on the right and small on the left.  The left
pleural effusion has elements of loculation (2:21) without abnormal
enhancement , likely secondary to the recent surgery.  There is no
pneumothorax.  There is near complete collapse of the entire right lower lobe
with minimal dependent atelectasis in the left lung. These findings have
progressed since the pre-operative  CT scan of ___. Ground glass opacity
within the right middle lobe, likely represents atelectasis vs pneumonitis.

Multiple mediastinal lymphadenopathy is present, prevascular, right
paratracheal regions, maximally measuring 11 mm in the prevascular region
(2:20), not significantly changed since the prior study.  Incidentally noted
is fat density lesion within the inter-atrial septa, which may represent an
inter-atrial lipoma or fatty hypertrophy of the septum.  An epicardial lymph
node measuring 8 mm (2:40) is seen along the left lateral aspect of the
pericardium.  Mild atherosclerotic calcification of the thoracic aorta and the
coronary arteries are noted.  The ascending thoracic aorta maximally measures
3.5 cm. Incidental note is made of a hypoattenuating left thyroid nodule
measuring 2.1 x 1.6 cm (2:25).

CT OF THE ABDOMEN WITH ORAL AND INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST:
The liver demonstrates
homogeneous attenuation without focal lesions or intrahepatic biliary
dilatation.  The gallbladder is mildly distended with multiple gallstones. 
There is a moderate amount of free fluid along the inferior margin of the
liver extending into the pericholecystic region and tracking along the right
paracolic gutter into the pelvis.   The patient is status post splenectomy
with multiple enhancing rounded densities within the left upper quadrant,
compatible with splenosis.  There is a hypoattenuating fluid collection
(301:34) with peripheral rim enhancement, measuring 6.5 x 1.5 x 1.3 cm in the
left upper quadrant.  Mild fat stranding in the adjacent tail of pancreas,
could be a secondary to inflammation, however superimposed acute pancreatitis
cannot be excluded. There has been interval removal of a pigtail catheter
which was present in this collection.  Minimal amount of fat stranding and
fluid is seen along the entire left paracolic gutter, which likely relates to
the patient's recent left hemicolectomy.

The stomach and small bowel loops are unremarkable without evidence of bowel
wall thickening, obstruction, with the contrast reaching the right lower
quadrant ileostomy.  Incidental note is made of multiple duodenal diverticula
involving the second and third portion of the duodenum. Nasogastric tube ends
in the gastric body.

Both adrenal glands are unremarkable.  Both kidneys show symmetric
opacification and excretion of contrast without evidence of
hydroureteronephrosis.

There is no intra-abdominal free air.  The abdominal aorta demonstrates
diffuse severe atherosclerotic calcification without aneurysmal dilation.
Extensive calcification is seen in the iliac arteries, with significant
narrowing of the left external iliac and celiac axis. The celiac axis branches
are reconstituted via collaterals from the SMA. Significant narrowing of
bilateral common femoral arteries are noted.

CT OF THE PELVIS WITH ORAL AND INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST:
The patient is status
post left hemicolectomy with a diverting ileostomy.  The visualized portion of
the large bowel appears unremarkable.  The pelvic loops of small bowel are
unremarkable except for mild focal thickening of a short segment of small
bowel loop in the pelvis adjacent to left pelvic drain.

Left pelvic drain loops within the pelvis with tip terminating in the left
flank. Minimal subcutaneous soft tissue stranding is present in the left flank
around the left drainage catheter, however no drainable fluid collections or
abscesses are detected.  Small amount of pelvic free fluid is present.  The
uterus and adnexa are unremarkable.  The urinary bladder is empty with a Foley
catheter in place, and air visualized within the dome of the bladder relates
to the catheterization.

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES AND SOFT TISSUES:
The patient is status post spinal
instrumentation at L4/L5 level.  The changes of the thoracolumbar spine are
present, worse at  L4-5 and L5-S1 levels.  There is mild grade 1
anterolisthesis on L4-L5 level.

"}, {"Note ID": "19689280-RR-28", "Modality": "CT", "Region": "Chest", "Exam Name": "CT CHEST W/CONTRAST", "Report": "TECHNIQUE:
MDCT helical acquisition was performed from the thoracic inlet to
the pubic symphysis following the uneventful administration of oral and IV
contrast.  Multiplanar reformations were provided.

CT CHEST WITH IV CONTRAST:
The patient has an endotracheal tube which
terminates 3 cm above the carina in satisfactory position.  Left internal
jugular line terminates in the mid superior vena cava.  A right upper
extremity PICC has been removed.  There is similar degree of right greater
than left pleural effusions, causing compressive atelectasis and right lower
lobe collapse as well as left lower lobe subsegmental atelectasis.  The
remainder of the airways are patent.  There is a similar appearance of small
focus of ground-glass opacity in the right middle lobe.

Mediastinal lymph nodes previously described are again both prominent in size
and number; aorticopulmonary window lymph node measures 2.0 x 1.0 cm (2:18). 
There is lipomatous hypertrophy of the interatrial septum.  Prominent
epicardial lymph node is again seen (2:38).  There is atherosclerotic
calcification of the thoracic aorta and the coronary arteries.  The previously
described thyroid nodule is again seen (2:2).

CT ABDOMEN WITH IV CONTRAST:
A nasogastric tube terminates in the stomach. 
The patient is post left colectomy, with a colorectal anastomosis which
appears intact (2:100).  A diverting ileostomy in the right lower quadrant
appears unchanged, and there is no evidence of bowel obstruction.  Oral
contrast reaches the ileostomy site.

In the left upper quadrant, the CT-guided drain has been removed at time of
colectomy, and the rim-enhancing abscess is approximately stable in size,
measuring 6.5 x 1.1 x 1.6 cm.  There is a similar degree of mild ascites in
the left upper quadrant tracking along the left paracolic gutter.  There is a
moderate degree of ascites in the right paracolic gutter.  

A surgically placed in the left lower quadrant loops superiorly and terminates
in the left mid abdomen (2:70).  Fluid has accumulated in the left lower
quadrant abdominal wall at site where drain enters, measuring 8.0 x 3.6 cm. 
This is somewhat ill-defined and may represent postoperative collection
measuring, with currently no thick enhancing rim to suggest abscess (2:91).

Inferior to the last midline abdominal wall staple, a 2.6 x 1.8 cm soft tissue
defect is filled with heterogenous material which may represent packing
material/gauze or debris, possibly even infected.  This determination could be
made on visual inspection, as it reaches the skin surface.

The liver is similar in appearance, without focal lesion.  The gallbladder is
not dilated, but again demonstrates multiple gallstones.  The patient is
status post remote splenectomy.  Mild fat stranding in the region of the tail
of the pancreas is unchanged and most likely reactive in this setting unless
there is concern for pancreatitis.  The kidneys enhance normally and excrete
IV contrast normally.  There is no intra-abdominal free air.

Atherosclerotic disease of the abdominal aorta and its branches is unchanged.

CT PELVIS WITH IV CONTRAST:
The colorectal anastomosis appears unremarkable. 
The uterus appears within normal limits.  No pelvic abscess is identified. 
There is no pelvic or inguinal lymphadenopathy.  Small amount of pelvic free
fluid is similar to the prior study.  The urinary bladder demonstrates Foley
catheter and expected air.

Osseous structures again demonstrate instrumentation of the posterior elements
at L4-L5.  Degenerative changes at the lumbosacral junction appear similar,
with grade I anterolisthesis of L4 on L5.

"}, {"Note ID": "19689280-RR-31", "Modality": "Drainage", "Region": "Abdomen", "Exam Name": "CT PERITINEAL DRAIN EXCLUDING APPENDICEAL", "Report": "CT-GUIDED DRAINAGE:
Informed consent was obtained from the patient's healthcare proxy over the
phone with two witnesses present.  The patient was placed supine on the CT
table in a slightly right decubital position and limited preprocedural CT scan
was obtained for procedure planning purposes.  This demonstrates persistent
moderate-sized right effusion with compression atelectasis involving portions
of the right lower lobe as well as small left effusion and lower lobe
atelectasis.  Clips from prior surgical procedure are noted and an orogastric
tube in place.  The overall size of the thick-rimmed left upper quadrant
collection abutting the greater curvature of the stomach displays no short
interval change from the most recent CT examination with surrounding
inflammation of the fat.  

The overlying skin was prepped and draped in normal sterile fashion and
approximately 10 mL of 1% lidocaine was instilled into the local subcutaneous
tissues for local anesthesia.  Subsequently, under CT fluoroscopic guidance,
an 18-gauge ___ needle was advanced using an anterolateral approach into
the collection with aspiration of purulent material of approximately 3 cc
which was sent for culture and sensitivity.  The needle was removed and a
0.035 ___ wire was then advanced and coiled within the collection. 
Sequential tract dilatation with 6 and 8 ___ dilators was then performed. 
Using Seldinger technique, an 8 ___ ___ pigtail catheter was advanced
into the collection with the pigtail appropriately positioned as confirmed by
CT fluoroscopic images.  An additional 20 mL of bloody purulent material was
aspirated and discarded.  The catheter was placed to JP suction and secured to
the skin using a StatLock device and a sterile dressing was applied.  The
patient tolerated the procedure well with no immediate post-procedure
complications.

The patient came down to the CT interventional suite on Versed and fentanyl
which was continued to be administered by the ICU nurse during the procedure
for patient comfort.  The procedure was performed by Dr. ___ supervised
by Dr. ___ attending radiologist, who was present and participating
throughout.

"}, {"Note ID": "19689280-RR-45", "Modality": "CT", "Region": "Chest", "Exam Name": "CT CHEST W/CONTRAST", "Report": "TECHNIQUE:

atelectatic changes.  The patient is status post cholecystectomy.  There are

the left renal cortex is bulging in the interpolar region.  A lesion in this
location is possible and would not be detectable with non-contrast study.
There is no abdominal ascites.

CT PELVIS WITHOUT IV CONTRAST:
There is a large soft tissue abnormality
measuring approximately 7 x 5 cm in the axial plane centered along the course
of the sigmoid colon.  The evaluation of this abnormality is extremely limited
due to the absence of oral contrast in this location as well as absence of IV
contrast.  There is probable extraluminal air and there is a small adjacent
fluid collection measuring 1.1 x 0.9 cm (2:62).  Given the limitations of the
study, it is unclear whether the uterus is involved, and if an area of
multiple small pockets of air is extraluminal or intraluminal  (2:68)  The

fractures of the right superior and inferior pubic rami.  Hardware material

severe degenerative changes throughout the lumbar spine, sacroiliac and pubic

probably represents a small abscess and of localized free air probably

FINDINGS:


LOWER CHEST:
Please refer to separate report of CT chest performed on the same

ABDOMEN:


HEPATOBILIARY:
The liver is shrunken and nodular in contour compatible with
cirrhosis.  There are no evidence of suspicious hepatic lesions.  There is
mild periportal edema.  No intra or extrahepatic biliary ductal dilatation. 
Vicarious excretion of contrast media is again noted within the gallbladder
which is otherwise unremarkable.  Perihepatic ascites is slightly decreased in
comparison to prior.

PANCREAS:
There is mild fatty atrophy of the pancreatic parenchyma.  The
pancreas demonstrates normal attenuation throughout, without evidence of focal
lesions or pancreatic ductal dilatation.  There is no peripancreatic
stranding.

SPLEEN:
The spleen shows normal size and attenuation throughout.  A punctate
hypodensity within the splenic parenchyma is not well visualized on the prior
study but is of doubtful clinical significance (02:17).

ADRENALS:
The right and left adrenal glands are normal in size and shape.

URINARY:
The kidneys are of normal and symmetric size with normal nephrogram. 
There is no evidence of focal renal lesions or hydronephrosis.  Multiple
bilateral simple renal cysts measure up to 6 cm in the lower pole of the right
kidney, stable from prior.  Sub-centimeter hypodensities in the bilateral
renal parenchyma are too small to characterize but also likely represent
simple cysts.  There is no perinephric abnormality.

GASTROINTESTINAL:
There is a small amount of pneumoperitoneum, probably
decreased in comparison to prior. Enteric tube terminates in the mid gastric
body.  Stomach is otherwise unremarkable but not well assessed on CT.  There
are several duodenal diverticula, similar in appearance to prior (02:39,
34-36).  There is no small bowel obstruction.  Patient is now status post
partial sigmoid resection with creation of a sigmoid colostomy.  The sigmoid
staple line is unremarkable. Sigmoid colon at the ostomy site demonstrate mild
mural thickening which most likely represents postsurgical change (02:59).  In
comparison to the prior study there is worsened wall thickening involving the
cecum, ascending and proximal transverse colon which may in part be related to
under-distention and portal colopathy.  Retained oral contrast media is noted
within a thickened terminal ileum and throughout the colon, most conspicuous
where retained within multiple diverticula (602:16).  The overall amount of
intra-abdominal ascites is improved from prior with residual mesenteric edema,
trace fluid and some omental thickening along the course of the right colon.
There is no evidence of extraluminal contrast within the abdomen or pelvis. 
The previously seen oral contrast containing collection is markedly smaller
now only containing a small amount of simple fluid.  A drainage catheter
terminates in the left lower quadrant.  No new abscesses or suspicious fluid
collections are identified.

PELVIS:
Urinary bladder is decompressed with a Foley catheter.  There is trace
free fluid in the pelvis.

REPRODUCTIVE ORGANS:
Prostate and seminal vesicles are grossly unremarkable.

LYMPH NODES:
No suspicious lymphadenopathy within the abdomen or pelvis. 
Conspicuous porta hepatic nodes are likely reactive the setting of chronic
liver disease.

VASCULAR:
No abdominal aortic aneurysm.  There is extensive both calcified and
noncalcified plaque involving the abdominal aorta and its great branches. 
There is mild ectasia of the infrarenal abdominal measuring up to 2.5 cm.

BONES:
No worrisome osseous lesion or acute fracture.  Moderate to severe
multilevel degenerative changes in the lumbar spine are stable.

SOFT TISSUES:
Postsurgical changes are noted in the anterior abdominal wall
cleaning skin staples.  There is diffuse body wall anasarca.

atherosclerotic calcifications in the coronary arteries, mild in the aorta and

"}, {"Note ID": "18525603-RR-12", "Modality": "Drainage", "Region": "Abdomen", "Exam Name": "PERC IMAGE GUID FLUID COLLECT DRAIN W CATH(ABSC,HEMA/SEROMA;LYMPHOCELE,CYST);PERIT/RETROPERITONEAL", "Report": "PROCEDURE:
Ultrasound-guided transvaginal drainage of pelvic collection.

TECHNIQUE:
The risks, benefits, and alternatives of the procedure were
explained to the patient. After a detailed discussion, informed written
consent was obtained. A pre-procedure timeout using three patient identifiers
was performed per ___ protocol.

The patient was placed in a lithotomy  position on the US scan table. Limited
preprocedure transvaginal ultrasound was performed to localize the collection.
The scans demonstrated a 1cm communication from the sigmoid colon to the
abscess and a distal communication from the abscess to the vagina.  Based on
the ultrasound findings an appropriate skin entry site for the drain placement
was chosen. The site was marked. Local anesthesia was administered with 1%
Lidocaine solution.

Using continuous sonographic guidance, ___ drainage catheter was
advanced via trocar technique into the collection. A sample of fluid was
aspirated, confirming catheter position within the collection. The pigtail was
deployed. The position of the pigtail was confirmed within the collection via
ultrasound.

Approximately 30 cc of tan, purulent fluid was drained with a sample sent for
microbiology evaluation. The catheter was secured by a StatLock. The catheter
was attached to suction bulb. Sterile dressing was applied.

The procedure was tolerated well, and there were no immediate post-procedural
complications.

SEDATION:
Moderate sedation was provided by administering divided doses of 4
mg Versed and 200 mcg fentanyl throughout the total intra-service time of 18
minutes during which patient's hemodynamic parameters were continuously
monitored by an independent trained radiology nurse.

FINDINGS:
The pelvic fluid collection is seen with ultrasound.  There is a wide open
connection between the fluid collection and the adjacent sigmoid colon.  This
area of connection measures 7 mm in diameter.Fistulous communication to the
vagina also demonstarted.
